chief_complaint: 52-year-old man presented with acute myocardial infarction followed by ischemic stroke.
history: The patient presented with acute myocardial infarction followed by ischemic stroke diagnosed within hours, representing metachronous cardio-cerebral infarction. He demonstrated significant cognitive and language impairments that limited participation in a standard outpatient cardiac rehabilitation program.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG: acute myocardial infarction; Bloods: not provided; Imaging: not provided.
